plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim semiconductor (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD) to reduce concentration.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet single-name limit and increase cash.
Buy Microsoft (MSFT) as a high-quality tech addition.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability.
Hold a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and exit others (SPAL) to lower risk.
Reduce concentration in semiconductors (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD).
Trim broad index (SPY) to meet single-name limits and free up capital.
Buy Microsoft (MSFT) and AMD for quality tech exposure.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
Aim for risk-first growth with a target of around 2.5% monthly return.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly return by reducing leverage and concentration. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and some leveraged positions, prioritizing risk management.

plan: Increase leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to maximize upside potential.
Add to semiconductors (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD) for concentrated growth.
Trim broad index (SPY) and income (QQQI) to fund growth areas.
Buy AMD as a high-conviction tech addition.
Maintain a small cash buffer to stay invested.
Accept higher potential drawdowns for an aggressive growth target of ~4% monthly.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for ~4% monthly return by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-scoring tech names and leveraged ETFs. It accepts significantly higher potential drawdowns and the daily rebalancing decay of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and volatile sectors (SMH, CRWD).
Trim broad index (SPY) to reduce overall market exposure.
Significantly increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI).
Hold cryptocurrency (COIN) but do not add further.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer for stability.
Prioritize reliable monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% monthly return through distributions, prioritizing capital preservation. It minimizes leverage and growth-oriented assets, shifting towards income-generating ETFs.
